---
title: "03_beta_diversity"
author: "Zulema Carracedo Lorenzo"
date: "2025-03-10"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
#summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
#plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

#Load packages
```{r}
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(vegan) #for permanova
library(pairwiseAdonis) #for potshoc
library(tibble)
library(umap)
library(metagMisc)
library(microbiome)
library(RColorBrewer)

#for exporting tables 
library(flextable)
library(officer)

```

#Load phyloseq object
```{r}
load("./results/phyloseq/ps_filtered.RData") 

```

#Subset data

```{r}
#We will only use the samples from the bulk soil

ps_filtered_bulk <-  subset_samples(ps_filtered, Sample_type %in% "bulk_soil")
ps_filtered_bulk <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps_filtered_bulk) >0, ps_filtered_bulk)

```

##Keep only informative ASVs

#I will use Chrats filtering, it keeps those ASvs with relative abundance higher than 0.001%

#### [1. Non-filtered] ####

```{r}

#non-filtered ps object
ps_filtered_bulk # Initial 48,027 ASVs

#The code for filtering was writted by Chrats in the 01_Preprocessing script from the Brassicaceae Fam Exp. I took the modified version from Marcela's PS_microbiome repository as it was more clear

```

#### [2. Soft-filtering - By water regime] ####

```{r}
#Preparing data
tax_table(ps_filtered_bulk)<-cbind(rownames(tax_table(ps_filtered_bulk)), tax_table(ps_filtered_bulk)) #make new with rownames
colnames(tax_table(ps_filtered_bulk))[1] <- "ASV" #name column ASV

#Select the condition by which I will be filtering -> water_regime
conds_WR <- unique(sample_data(ps_filtered_bulk)$water_regime)

#1) filter out mean relative abundance lower than 0.0001% per water regime 
#The threshold has to be 10x lower for bacteria because there's not a lot of super abundant ASVs compared to Fungi 

phyloseq_RA_per_WR<-list() #empty list

#make one ps per DES_group and keep only those ASVs which have a relabundance > than 0.0001%
#In this case a DES_group it's each Experiment

for(i in 1:length(conds_WR)){
  meta <- ps_filtered_bulk@sam_data[which(sample_data(ps_filtered_bulk)$water_regime == conds_WR[i]),] 
  x16S_temp<-subset_samples(ps_filtered_bulk, sample_data(ps_filtered_bulk)$water_regime %in% conds_WR[i])
  x16S_temp = prune_taxa(taxa_sums(x16S_temp) != 0, x16S_temp) 
  x16S_temp.prop = transform_sample_counts(x16S_temp, function(x) x / sum(x) ) #relabundance
  filt1 = filter_taxa(x16S_temp.prop, function(x) mean(x) > 0.0001, TRUE)
  
  phyloseq_RA_per_WR[[i]]<-filt1
  
}

#lists with taxa (ASV) names
ASVs_all_RA<-lapply(phyloseq_RA_per_WR, function(x) rownames(x@otu_table) )
length(unique(unlist(ASVs_all_RA)))  #1,912 ASVs

#make it a single ps object pruning those ASVs selected before 
ps_filtered_bulk_soft <- ps_filtered_bulk %>%
  phyloseq::subset_taxa(
    ASV %in% unique(unlist(ASVs_all_RA)))

ps_filtered_bulk_soft # Final 1912 ASVs
sum(taxa_sums(ps_filtered_bulk_soft))/sum(taxa_sums(ps_filtered_bulk)) # 84.8% of original reads kept

#clean
rm(ASVs_all_RA, filt1, x16S_temp, x16S_temp.prop, meta, phyloseq_RA_per_WR, i, conds_WR)



```

#### [3. Soft-filtering - By Water Regime & Treatment] ####

```{r}

#3) filter out mean relative abundance of 0.0001% per combination of Water Regime & Treatment

#make new column with unique groups by the combination of Water Regime & Treatment
sample_data(ps_filtered_bulk)$DES_groups <- paste0(sample_data(ps_filtered_bulk)$water_regime, "_", phyloseq::sample_data(ps_filtered_bulk)$treatment)
conds_WR_TR <-unique(sample_data(ps_filtered_bulk)$DES_groups)

phyloseq_per_combination<-list() #empty list

#make one ps per DES_group and keep only those ASVs which have a relabundance > than 0.0001%
for(i in 1:length(conds_WR_TR)){
  meta <- ps_filtered_bulk@sam_data[which(sample_data(ps_filtered_bulk)$DES_groups == conds_WR_TR[i]),] 
  x16S_temp<-subset_samples(ps_filtered_bulk, sample_data(ps_filtered_bulk)$DES_groups %in% conds_WR_TR[i])
  x16S_temp = prune_taxa(taxa_sums(x16S_temp) != 0, x16S_temp) 
  x16S_temp.prop = transform_sample_counts(x16S_temp, function(x) x / sum(x) ) #relabundance
  filt1 = filter_taxa(x16S_temp.prop, function(x) mean(x) > 0.0001, TRUE)
  
  phyloseq_per_combination[[i]]<-filt1
  
}

#lists with taxa (ASV) names
ASVs_all_RA<-lapply(phyloseq_per_combination, function(x) rownames(x@otu_table) )
length(unique(unlist(ASVs_all_RA))) #2,760 unique ASVs

#make it a single ps object pruning those ASVs selected before 
ps_filtered_bulk_soft_comb_WR_TR <- ps_filtered_bulk %>%
  phyloseq::subset_taxa(
    ASV %in% unique(unlist(ASVs_all_RA)))

#check
sum(taxa_sums(ps_filtered_bulk_soft_comb_WR_TR))/sum(taxa_sums(ps_filtered_bulk)) # common set represents 88.8% of all reads  
ps_filtered_bulk_soft_comb_WR_TR #2,360 ASVs final 

#clean
rm(x16S_temp, filt1, meta, x16S_temp.prop, ASVs_all_RA, i)

```



#### [5. Hard-filtering - By Water Regime & Treatment] ####

```{r}
# 4) filter out mean relative abundance of 0.001% & presence in at least 50% of samples
# per combination of Water Regime & Treatment

phyloseq_hard<-list() #empty list
conds_WR_TR <- unique(sample_data(ps_filtered_bulk_soft_comb_WR_TR)$DES_groups) #The base is the ps_filtered_bulk_soft_comb_WR_ST ps object we just did

#make a for loop to split ps in all the DES groups
for(i in 1:length(conds_WR_TR)){
  #counts number of zeroes per condition
  #this is the threshold value
  meta <- ps_filtered_bulk_soft_comb_WR_TR@sam_data[which(sample_data(ps_filtered_bulk_soft_comb_WR_TR)$DES_groups == conds_WR_TR[i]),] 
  Bac_temp<-subset_samples(ps_filtered_bulk_soft_comb_WR_TR, #subset into each DES_group category
            sample_data(ps_filtered_bulk_soft_comb_WR_TR)$DES_groups %in% conds_WR_TR[i]) 
  Bac_temp =prune_taxa(taxa_sums(Bac_temp) != 0, Bac_temp) #remove all those ASVs that have 0 reads
  
  phyloseq_hard[[i]]<-Bac_temp
  
}

#Now, use the function phyloseq_filter_prevalence() on each of the DES groups to keep only those 
#ASVs that are present in at least 50% of the samples
prevalence_hard <-lapply(phyloseq_hard, function(ps){
  
  ps_byPrev <- phyloseq_filter_prevalence(ps, prev.trh = 0.50, #function from metagMisc package
                                          abund.trh = NULL)
  #final filtered ps
  return(ps_byPrev)
})

#Get names of ASVs
ASVnames <-lapply(prevalence_hard, function(x) rownames(x@otu_table))

#See how many unique ASVs are per experiment
length(unique(unlist(ASVnames))) #2,471 unique ASVs

#Get a vector of the ASV names
ASV_tokeep <- unique(unlist(ASVnames))

#finally, make it a single ps object keeping that list of unique ASVs
ps_filtered_bulk_comb_WR_TR_hard <- prune_taxa(ASV_tokeep, ps_filtered_bulk)

#check
sum(taxa_sums(ps_filtered_bulk_comb_WR_TR_hard))/sum(taxa_sums(ps_filtered_bulk)) # common set represents 87.35% of all reads  

#clean
rm(phyloseq_hard, conds_WR_TR, meta, Bac_temp, prevalence_hard, ASVnames, ASV_tokeep)
```


### Normalize data 
```{r}
#Cumulative Sum Scaling (CSS) is a median-like quantile normalization which corrects differences in sampling depth (library size). While standard relative abundance (fraction/percentage) normalization re-scales all samples to the same total sum (100%), CSS keeps a variation in total counts between samples.

#make a list of our ps objects
ps_l <- list(non_filt=ps_filtered_bulk, ByWR=ps_filtered_bulk_soft, soft=ps_filtered_bulk_soft_comb_WR_TR, hard=ps_filtered_bulk_comb_WR_TR_hard)

#normalize them with CSS 
ps_l_CSS <- lapply(ps_l, function(ps) phyloseq_transform_css(ps, norm=TRUE, log=TRUE))
#check
sample_sums(ps_l_CSS$non_filt) #good
sample_sums(ps_l_CSS$ByWR)

#remove single ps to release space in the environment
rm(ps_filtered_bulk, ps_filtered_bulk_soft, ps_filtered_bulk_soft_comb_WR_TR, ps_filtered_bulk_comb_WR_TR_hard)

```

#Saving phyloseque objects
```{r}
#I decided I will work with ps_filtered_bulk_comb_WR_TR_hard Give it an easier name and save it 

ps_hard_filt_unnorm <- ps_filtered_bulk_comb_WR_TR_hard
save(ps_hard_filt_unnorm, file = "./Results/phyloseq/ps_hard_filt_unnorm_March25.RData")
load(file = "./Results/phyloseq/ps_hard_filt_unnorm_March25.RData")

#save also the CSS normalized object
ps_hard_filt_CSS <- ps_l_CSS$hard
save(ps_hard_filt_CSS, file = "./Results/phyloseq/ps_hard_filt_CSS_March25.RData")
load(file = "./Results/phyloseq/ps_hard_filt_CSS_March25.RData")

```


# Beta-diversity plots
## NMDS
```{r}
# calculate NMDS ordination for the different ps objects

  set.seed(300)
  NMDS_l <- lapply(ps_l_CSS, function (x)
          ordinate(x,
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray",
                   try=200,
                   autotransform=TRUE))

 save(NMDS_l , file="./results/r_objects/beta_diversity/NMDS_l_MArch25.RData") 
 load(file="./results/r_objects/beta_diversity/NMDS_l.RData")

 #NMDS plots
 
 PCoA_plot_calculation_l <- function(physeq_l, ordination_output) {
  
    untitled_plot_l<-mapply(function(x, y)
    phyloseq::plot_ordination(physeq = x, 
                              ordination = y,
                              color ="treatment",
                              shape = "water_regime") + 
      theme_classic() +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold")) +
      theme(legend.position="right"),
    x = physeq_l,
    y = ordination_output,
    SIMPLIFY = FALSE)
 


titles_l <- names(untitled_plot_l)



Plot_l<- mapply(function (z,w)
  z + ggtitle(w),
  z = untitled_plot_l,
  w = titles_l,
  SIMPLIFY = FALSE)

return(Plot_l)
}

NMDS_plots <-  PCoA_plot_calculation_l(ps_l_CSS, NMDS_l)

#save plots
# create a list of plot names
plot_names <- c("non_filt_March25.png", "soft_WR_March25.png", "soft_WR_TR_March25.png",  "hard_WR_ST_March25.png")

lapply(seq_along(NMDS_plots), function(i) {
  ggsave(plot_names[i], plot = NMDS_plots[[i]],
         path = "./results/plots/beta_diversity",
         width = 30, height = 20, units = "cm")
})




#### Make plots only for hard filtering phyloseq object ###

  set.seed(300)
  NMDS <- ordinate(ps_hard_filt_CSS,
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray",
                   try=200,
                   autotransform=TRUE)
  
  
  plot_ordination(physeq = ps_hard_filt_CSS, 
                              ordination = NMDS,
                              color ="treatment",
                              shape = "water_regime") + 
      theme_classic() +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold")) +
      theme(legend.position="right")+
      geom_point(size =4)
  
## calculate only on WW samples
  
 ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW <-  subset_samples(ps_hard_filt_CSS, water_regime== "WW")
  
  set.seed(300)
  NMDS <- ordinate(ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW,
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray",
                   try=200,
                   autotransform=TRUE)
  
  
  plot_ordination(physeq = ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW, 
                              ordination = NMDS,
                              color ="treatment") + 
      theme_classic() +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold")) +
      theme(legend.position="right")+
      geom_point(size =4)
  

  
    ## calculate only on WW samples
  
 ps_hard_filt_CSS_DR <-  subset_samples(ps_hard_filt_CSS, water_regime== "DR")
  
  set.seed(300)
  NMDS <- ordinate(ps_hard_filt_CSS_DR,
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray",
                   try=200,
                   autotransform=TRUE)
  
  
  plot_ordination(physeq = ps_hard_filt_CSS_DR, 
                              ordination = NMDS,
                              color ="treatment") + 
      theme_classic() +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold")) +
      theme(legend.position="right")+
      geom_point(size =4)
  
```

## UMAP

```{r}
# First transpose OTU table so that rows are samples and OTUs columns 

otu_table_l <- lapply(ps_l_CSS, function(x)
                                t(otu_table(x)))

# Calculate dissimilarity indexes with bray curtis method 

bray_l <- lapply(otu_table_l, function (x)
    vegdist(x, method = "bray"))

bray_matrix_l <- lapply(bray_l, function (x)
    as.matrix(x))

# Run UMAP on bray curtis dissimilarity matrix

UMAP_bray_l <- lapply(bray_matrix_l, function (x)
      umap(x))

save(UMAP_bray_l, file = "./results/r_objects/beta_diversity/UMAP_bray_l.RData")
load(file = "./results/r_objects/beta_diversity/UMAP_bray_l.RData")

head(UMAP_bray_l$non_filt$layout, 3) #layout is the matrix in which we are interested


# plot umap

layoutBray <- lapply(UMAP_bray_l, function (x)
      as.data.frame(x$layout))

colnames(layoutBray$non_filt) <-  c("UMAP1","UMAP2")
colnames(layoutBray$ByWR) <-  c("UMAP1","UMAP2")
colnames(layoutBray$soft) <-  c("UMAP1","UMAP2")
colnames(layoutBray$soft2) <-  c("UMAP1","UMAP2")
colnames(layoutBray$hard) <-  c("UMAP1","UMAP2")
colnames(layoutBray$hard2) <-  c("UMAP1","UMAP2")

umap_meta_non_filt <- cbind(layoutBray$non_filt, sample_data(ps_l_CSS$non_filt))
umap_meta_ByWR <- cbind(layoutBray$ByWR, sample_data(ps_l_CSS$ByWR))
umap_meta_soft <- cbind(layoutBray$soft, sample_data(ps_l_CSS$soft))
umap_meta_soft2 <- cbind(layoutBray$soft2, sample_data(ps_l_CSS$soft2))
umap_meta_hard <- cbind(layoutBray$hard, sample_data(ps_l_CSS$hard))
umap_meta_hard2 <- cbind(layoutBray$hard2, sample_data(ps_l_CSS$hard2))

# UMAP plot 

#non-filtered
umap_meta_non_filt$Treatment_comb <- paste0(sample_data(umap_meta_non_filt)$water_regime, "_", phyloseq::sample_data(umap_meta_non_filt)$treatment) 

  ggplot(umap_meta_non_filt, aes(x=UMAP1,y=UMAP2, colour=Treatment_comb, shape=Sample_type)) +
  geom_point(aes(color=Treatment_comb), size=5) +
  ggtitle("UMAP Bray-Curtis 16S / non-filtered")

ggsave("umap_plot_non_filt.png", path = "./results/plots/beta_diversity",
         width = 30, height = 20, units = "cm")

#soft WR
umap_meta_ByWR$Treatment_comb <- paste0(sample_data(umap_meta_ByWR)$water_regime, "_", phyloseq::sample_data(umap_meta_ByWR)$treatment) 

  ggplot(umap_meta_ByWR, aes(x=UMAP1,y=UMAP2, colour=Treatment_comb, shape=Sample_type)) +
  geom_point(aes(color=Treatment_comb), size=5) +
  ggtitle("UMAP Bray-Curtis 16S / soft WR")

ggsave("umap_plot_soft_WR.png", path = "./results/plots/beta_diversity",
         width = 30, height = 20, units = "cm")


MISSIMG Plots

#Hard WR-ST-T
umap_meta_hard2$Treatment_comb <- paste0(sample_data(umap_meta_hard2)$water_regime, "_", phyloseq::sample_data(umap_meta_hard2)$treatment) 

  ggplot(umap_meta_hard2, aes(x=UMAP1,y=UMAP2, colour=Treatment_comb, shape=Sample_type)) +
  geom_point(aes(color=Treatment_comb), size=5) +
  ggtitle("UMAP Bray-Curtis 16S / soft WR")

ggsave("umap_plot_hard_WR_ST_T.png", path = "./results/plots/beta_diversity",
         width = 30, height = 20, units = "cm")

```

## Plots for paper

```{r}

  set.seed(300)
  NMDS <- ordinate(ps_hard_filt_CSS,
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray",
                   try=200,
                   autotransform=TRUE)
  
  
  plot_ordination(physeq = ps_hard_filt_CSS, 
                              ordination = NMDS,
                              color ="treatment",
                              shape = "water_regime") + 
    scale_color_manual(values = c("#FFFF99", "#A6CEE3", "#B2DF8A", "#CAB2D6")) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c(21, 22) )+
      theme_classic() +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold")) +
      theme(legend.position="right")+
      geom_point(size =5)
  
  
table_NMDS <- as.data.frame(NMDS$points) #save NMDS values in a dataframe

metadata <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_hard_filt_CSS)) #save metadata in a dataframe

metadata <- cbind(metadata, table_NMDS) #add NMDS1 and NMDS2 columns to metadate

#arrange order of factor for ggplot
metadata$treatment <- factor(metadata$treatment, c("Control", "WCS417", "WCS315", "CHA0"))
metadata$water_regime <- factor(metadata$water_regime, c("WW", "DR"))

ggplot(metadata, aes(MDS1,MDS2, fill=treatment, shape = water_regime))+
    geom_point(size=8, stroke = 2)+
    scale_shape_manual(values = c(21, 22) )+
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#FFFF99", "#A6CEE3", "#B2DF8A", "#CAB2D6")) +
    guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
    #geom_mark_ellipse(expand = 0.03,aes(fill=Pseudomonas_strain)) +
    #xlab(paste0("PC1: ",percentVar[1],"% variance")) +
    #ylab(paste0("PC2: ",percentVar[2],"% variance")) +
    theme_bw(18) +
    labs(x = "NMDS1", y = "NMDS2", fill = "Pseudomonas strain",  shape = "Sample type") +
    theme(axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
          panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          panel.border = element_blank(),
          panel.background = element_blank()) 

 ggsave("NMDS_hardfilt_March25.pdf", width = 40, height = 25, units = "cm", dpi=800)
 
#Let's add the centroid info
metadata

centroids <- metadata %>%
  group_by( DES_groups) %>%
  summarize(meanNMDS1 = mean(MDS1), meanNMDS2 = mean(MDS2))

#add centroid data to metadata
metadata <- merge(metadata, centroids, by = "DES_groups")


# Plot NMDS with centroids
ggplot(nmds_scores, aes(x = MDS1.x, y = MDS2.x, color = treatment, shape = water_regime)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +  # Points for each sample
  geom_point(data = centroids, aes(x = meanNMDS1, y = meanNMDS2, color = treatment, shape = water_regime), 
             size = 5, shape = 17) +  # Centroids with different shape
  geom_text(data = centroids, vjust = -1, size = 5) +  # Label centroids
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "NMDS Plot with Treatment Centroids") +
  theme(legend.position = "right")


centroids$treatment <- factor(centroids$treatment, c("Control", "WCS417", "WCS315", "CHA0"))
centroids$water_regime <- factor(centroids$water_regime, c("WW", "DR"))
control <- #648FFF
WCS417 <- #DC267F
WCS315 <- #FE6100
CHA0 <- #FFB000

ggplot(metadata,aes(MDS1,MDS2,color=treatment,fill=treatment, shape = water_regime)) +
  geom_point(size=3, alpha=1) + 
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21, 22) )+
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#648FFF", "#DC267F", "#FE6100", "#FFB000")) +
  geom_segment(aes(x=meanNMDS1 , y=meanNMDS2, xend=MDS1, yend=MDS2, color = treatment), alpha = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#648FFF", "#DC267F", "#FE6100", "#FFB000")) +
  geom_point(aes(x=meanNMDS1 , y=meanNMDS2), size = 10)+
  labs(x = "NMDS1", y = "NMDS2", fill = "Pseudomonas strain",  shape = "Sample type") +
    theme_bw() + 
    theme(text = element_text(size = 20))


ggsave("NMDS_centroids_March25.pdf", width = 40, height = 25, units = "cm", dpi=800)
 
 
```

2.3. Permanovas

##Permanova for papers

```{r}
set.seed(303848)
permanova <- adonis2(formula = phyloseq::distance(t(otu_table(ps_hard_filt_CSS)), method="bray") ~ treatment + water_regime,
              data = as(sample_data(ps_hard_filt_CSS),"data.frame"), # changing with as.data.frame is insufficient
              permutations = 999, by = "margin")

#save output 
# Convert output to data frame
permanova_df <- as.data.frame(permanova)
permanova_df$Factor <- rownames(permanova_df)
permanova_df <- permanova_df[, c("Factor", setdiff(names(permanova_df), "Factor"))]

# Round values
permanova_df$`Pr(>F)` <- formatC(permanova_df$`Pr(>F)`, format = "f", digits = 3)
permanova_df$R2 <- round(permanova_df$R2, 3)
permanova_df$F <- round(permanova_df$F, 2)
permanova_df$SumOfSqs <- round(permanova_df$SumOfSqs, 2)

# Create styled flextable
permanova_ft <- flextable(permanova_df) |>
  autofit() |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  bold(i = ~ `Pr(>F)` < 0.05, part = "body") |>
  set_header_labels(
    Factor = "Factor", Df = "DF", SumOfSqs = "Sum Sq",
    R2 = "R²", F = "F-value", `Pr(>F)` = "p-value"
  )

# Export to Word
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table: PERMANOVA Results (Bray-Curtis Dissimilarity)", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(permanova_ft)

print(doc, target = "PERMANOVA_brays_result.docx")


#Now perform posthoc test 
# Distance matrix (use the same as in your main PERMANOVA)
dist_mat <- phyloseq::distance(t(otu_table(ps_hard_filt_CSS)), method = "bray")


# Pairwise adonis by treatment
pairwise_results <- pairwise.adonis2(
  dist_mat ~ treatment,
  data = metadata,
  permutations = 999,
  p.adjust.m = "bonferroni"  # or "fdr" for less conservative adjustment
)

#save table for publication 

# Convert results to data frame
pairwise_df <- do.call(rbind, pairwise_results[-1])  # skip $parent_call
pairwise_df$Comparison <- names(pairwise_results)[-1]
pairwise_df <- pairwise_df[, c("Comparison", names(pairwise_df)[1:(ncol(pairwise_df)-1)])]

# Make table
pairwise_ft <- flextable(pairwise_df) |>
  autofit() |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  bold(i = ~ `Pr(>F)` < 0.05, part = "body") |>
  set_header_labels(
    Comparison = "Comparison",
    Df = "DF",
    SumOfSqs = "Sum Sq",
    R2 = "R²",
    F = "F-value",
    `Pr(>F)` = "p-value"
  )

# Export
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table: Pairwise PERMANOVA Results", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(pairwise_ft)

print(doc, target = "pairwise_PERMANOVA.docx")

```


## 2.3.1. Permanova for 16S samples

```{r}
#For now I will only test well-watered samples, as we don't have enough replicates for drought controls
#testing permanovas 16S 
set.seed(303848)
permanova <- adonis2(formula = phyloseq::distance(t(otu_table(ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW)), method="bray") ~ treatment  + Sample_type,
              data = as(sample_data(ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW),"data.frame"), # changing with as.data.frame is insufficient
              permutations = 999, by = "margin")



# Pairwise PERMANOVA 16S treatement factor
dist_bray_WW <- phyloseq::distance(t(otu_table(ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW)), method="bray")
set.seed(123)  
pairwise_adonis <- adonis_pairwise(x=metadata_WW, dd=dist_bray, group.var = "treatment")
pairwise_adonis$Adonis.tab
pairwise_adonis$Betadisper.tab

set.seed(123)  
EcolUtils::adonis.pair(dist_bray_WW, metadata_WW$treatment)


#If I test only WW-rhizosphere samples
ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW_rhizo <- subset_samples(ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW, Sample_type == "rhizosphere")

set.seed(303848)
permanova <- adonis2(formula = phyloseq::distance(t(otu_table(ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW)), method="bray") ~ treatment  ,
              data = as(sample_data(ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW),"data.frame"), # changing with as.data.frame is insufficient
              permutations = 999, by = "margin")

dist_bray_WW_Rhizo <- phyloseq::distance(t(otu_table(ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW_rhizo)), method="bray")
metadata_WW_rhizo <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW_rhizo))
metadata_WW_rhizo$treatment <- as.factor(metadata_WW_rhizo$treatment) #treatment needs to be a factor

set.seed(123)  
EcolUtils::adonis.pair(dist_bray_WW_Rhizo, metadata_WW_rhizo$treatment)



### Drought bulk soil ###

set.seed(303848)
permanova <- adonis2(formula = phyloseq::distance(t(otu_table(ps_hard_filt_CSS_DR_bulk)), method="bray") ~ treatment  ,
              data = as(sample_data(ps_hard_filt_CSS_DR_bulk),"data.frame"), # changing with as.data.frame is insufficient
              permutations = 999, by = "margin")

dist_bray_WW_Rhizo <- phyloseq::distance(t(otu_table(ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW_rhizo)), method="bray")
metadata_WW_rhizo <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_hard_filt_CSS_WW_rhizo))
metadata_WW_rhizo$treatment <- as.factor(metadata_WW_rhizo$treatment) #treatment needs to be a factor

set.seed(123)  
EcolUtils::adonis.pair(dist_bray_WW_Rhizo, metadata_WW_rhizo$treatment)


```
